rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current AADL scope before use in AGREE contracts.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes; use only visible ports and declared helpers.
When AGREE reports type errors, trace the first invalid identifier or helper on the same line and correct the root cause.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always reference only visible AADL features or previously declared helpers within the same annex.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations to prevent resolution errors.
Ensure that guarantee expressions have valid types by aligning helper declarations with their right-hand side expressions.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code unless they are explicitly imported via 'with' clauses.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid AADL component scopes.
Assignments in implementation scopes should only reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Guarantee statements in implementation scopes should not be used; place them in component type annexes instead.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or features in component type annexes unless they are properly declared as variables or outputs.
Do not write guarantee expressions that reference identifiers with unresolved types or invalid helper declarations.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
AGREE syntax and structure in general
